data.title: Ticket system core query layer: next-batch, list-descendants, list-epics, exists
data.description:
## Context

DSO plugin developers maintaining workflow scripts must understand ticket system internals to perform common operations: traversing epic-story-task hierarchies, checking blocking dependencies, and selecting the next batch of tasks. This knowledge is concentrated in scripts that bypass the ticket CLI and read .tickets-tracker/ directly — sprint-next-batch.sh (~963 lines with inline Python BFS), sprint-list-epics.sh (direct reducer invocation), and pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh (direct filesystem checks). The sprint orchestrator agent calls sprint-next-batch.sh directly, coupling it to the script's internal evolution rather than a stable, versioned CLI contract. This epic adds four high-value subcommands to the ticket CLI that eliminate the need for direct .tickets-tracker/ access, making the ticket system answer workflow-aware questions directly.

## Success Criteria

- Four new ticket CLI subcommands exist and are callable via the ticket CLI dispatcher: next-batch (batch selection with hierarchy walk, blocking checks, file-overlap serialization, greedy selection — replaces sprint-next-batch.sh/issue-batch.sh; preserves SKIPPED_DESIGN_AWAITING, SKIPPED_BLOCKED_STORY, SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING output tokens and --json format consumed by write-blackboard.sh), list-descendants (BFS tree walk returning child IDs by tier as a JSON object keyed by tier name), list-epics (epic listing with blocking status, child counts, priority sorting — replaces sprint-list-epics.sh; preserves tab-separated output format with blocker_ids field), and exists (O(1) ticket existence check via directory + CREATE-file presence without invoking the reducer — replaces direct filesystem checks in pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh)
- Each new subcommand has an integration test in tests/ that exercises the subcommand through the ticket CLI dispatcher against a synthetic .tickets-tracker/ fixture directory (following the existing pattern in test-sprint-next-batch.sh). Output equivalence means: same exit code contract (0=success, non-zero=failure; validate's 5-tier scored exit codes 0-4 are preserved where applicable), same top-level JSON keys and value correctness when output is structured, and same human-readable summary format when output is plain text. For next-batch specifically, tests assert correct blocking behavior (blocked story's child tasks excluded from batch) and correct file-overlap serialization (overlapping files produce exactly one SKIPPED_OVERLAP)
- A script author can retrieve next-batch, list-descendants, list-epics, and exists results using only ticket CLI commands — no knowledge of .tickets-tracker/ directory layout required — verified by integration tests exercising each subcommand end-to-end through the CLI dispatcher

## Dependencies

None (purely additive — no existing scripts are removed or refactored in this epic)

## Approach

One ticket-*.sh or ticket-*.py script per subcommand, dispatched from ticket-cli.sh, following the existing pattern. The exists subcommand checks directory existence + CREATE event file presence without invoking ticket-reducer.py, preserving sub-millisecond performance for pre-commit hooks. Downstream caller map: sprint/SKILL.md (12 sprint-next-batch.sh call sites), write-blackboard.sh (consumes --json format), debug-everything/SKILL.md, brainstorm/SKILL.md (calls sprint-list-epics.sh) — all remain on legacy scripts until Epic C refactors them.
